titleStmt.title: SII 26.477: original edition by P. R. Srinivasan
No. 477. (A.R. No. 454 of 1909.) MAṆAPPAḌAIVĪḌU, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT.

summary.p: West wall of the same maṇḍapa.
This inscription is damaged in several places and incomplete.

This records an order issued by the king at the instance of Ayyaṉ Maḻavarāyaṉ, granting land probably to the same temple mentioned in No. 476 above, to provide for worship and offerings in it. It appears that the land belonged as jīvitam to the Paḍaiyilār of the same place where the temple is situated viz., Ambalattāḍinallūr alias Maṇaṟpaḍaivīḍu in Śrīvallava-maṅgalam in Kīḻkaḷak-kūṟṟam, and it was situated in Nakka-maṅgalam in Kīḻvēmba-nāḍu. It is stated that the gift was a tax-free dēvadāna. Other details are lost.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . tribhuvaṉaccakravatti koṉeriṉmaikoṇṭāṉ kiḻkaḷakkūṟṟattu [śrīvallavaṉ]maṅkalattu vaṭakiḻ piṭākai Ampalattāṭi nallūrāṉa maṇappaṭaiviṭṭu koyil nampiceyvāṉukku
ttiruppaṭi māṟṟuḷḷiṭṭa veṇṭum nivantaṅkaḷukku Ivvūrp paṭaiyilārkku kiḻvempanāṭṭu nakkamaṅkalattil Ivarkaḷukkuc civitamāṉa nilattil Ivvāḻvārkkuk kārāṇmai kāṉamum tirunāmattu vilaiyum Āka Ivarkaḷ Ivvūr kū
kaṉūkku Iccilaivviṅkalattu vaṭakiḻ piṭākaiyil Ivvū kku kā tevatāṉa Iṟaiyiliyākak kuṭutta Ivvūr kaṭamai piṉpaṭi nilam muṉṟumāvum Ākanilam Araiye
ṉiyokamum taruvatāṉa Accum kāriyavārācciyum Uḻutāṉa ku lum nellum l cantivikkirakap peṟum poṉvariyum maṟṟum Eppeyarppaṭṭiṉavum Uṭpaṭap patiṉoṉṟāvatiṉe tirāmāṇṭu
Aiyyaṉ maḻavarāyaṉ namakkuc coṉṉamaiyil Ivvāḻvārkku tiruppaṭi māṟṟuḷḷiṭṭu veṇṭum nivantaṅkaḷukku Immaṇaṟpaṭaiviṭṭup paṭaiyilāṟkuk kiḻvempanāṭṭu nakkamaṅkalattil Ivarkaḷukkuc civitamāṉa nilattil Ivvā
laiyum Āka Ivvūr kaṭamai Iṟukkuṅ kolālk kuṭutta nilam yyakkaṉukku Iccivallavaṉ maṅkalattu vaṭakiḻpiṭākaiyil Uḻavāṉa tiruvalakkāṭṭil Ivvāḻvārkku kārāṇmai Uṭpaṭat tevatāṉa Iṟaiyiliyākak ku
Digital edition of SII 26.477 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
